# <Feature ID> <Feature Name>

## What This Is

State the workflow problem this feature solves and which lane owns it. Name the
primary artifact or CLI command if one exists.

## Current State

Use one of the project states: `proposed`, `approved`, `in_progress`, `done`,
`hold`, `rejected`, or `successor-lane`.

Record the latest verified behavior in a few bullets:

- implemented surface:
- active artifact:
- latest validation:
- human decision state:

## Next

Name the next concrete action and the friction it reduces. Keep this to one to
three actions, each with a clear success signal.

| Action | Why it matters | Success signal |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |

## Constraints / Risks

Collect non-approval, rights, source mutation, production/public, credential,
and artifact portability limits here instead of repeating them throughout the
page.

- production_candidate:
- rights_status:
- ignored/local artifact boundary:
- source/transcript mutation boundary:

## Artifacts / Readback

| Artifact | Path | Review status |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |

## Validation

List the narrow commands that prove this feature still works. Keep skipped or
environment-dependent checks separate from assertion failures.
